title: Tauri bundle config for Windows (NSIS)

context.current_targets: "all" — produces deb/rpm/appimage on Linux; on Windows would attempt msi+nsis but no per-OS config block exists.
context.goal: Explicit per-OS targets + windows installer config + icon set + sidecar binary declaration.

icons_needed: src-tauri/icons/icon.ico (Windows requires .ico for installer + exe; convert from existing icon.png if missing)
externalBin_note: Tauri appends -<triple><exe-suffix> automatically when locating the sidecar at bundle time. So binaries/whisper-cli + the per-triple copies (T04 step 10, T11 sidecar copy) cover all OSes.
steps: Convert icons/icon.png to icons/icon.ico (use ImageMagick: `convert icon.png -define icon:auto-resize=256,128,64,48,32,16 icon.ico` OR online; commit the .ico)
Replace bundle block in tauri.conf.json with bundle_block above
Verify externalBin path resolves: must have binaries/whisper-cli-<triple>{.exe} present at build time for each target OS
Run `npm run tauri build` on Linux to confirm config still parses and produces deb/appimage
(Defer Windows bundle test to T11 CI matrix)
acceptance: tauri.conf.json validates against schema (no editor warnings)
Linux release build produces dictate*.deb / *.AppImage as before
